title: Create verify.py ops module with checks 1 and 2
description: Create pypptx/ops/verify.py with the verify_pptx() function skeleton and implement check 1 (unfilled placeholder hint text) and check 2 (font size below minimum 12pt). verify_pptx() returns {errors, warnings, slide_count}. Check 1 matches case-insensitively against the 9 specified placeholder patterns, skipping shapes with no text frame and shapes whose stripped text is empty. Check 2 walks a:rPr[@sz] elements across all shapes, reports fonts below 1200 units (12pt) as errors, and excludes footer-region shapes where top > 90% of slide height.
acceptance_criteria: verify_pptx() exists in pypptx/ops/verify.py and returns a dict with errors, warnings, and slide_count keys
Check 1 detects all 9 placeholder patterns case-insensitively and reports each as an error naming the slide number and shape name
Check 1 does not trigger on shapes with no text frame
Check 1 does not trigger on shapes whose stripped text is empty
Check 2 detects fonts below 12pt (1200 units) and reports them as errors naming slide, shape, and actual size
Check 2 skips shapes where top > 90% of slide height
Check 2 does not trigger on fonts at or above 12pt

handoff_summary.completed: verify_pptx() created at .apm/skills/pypptx/pypptx/ops/verify.py. Check 1 detects all 9 placeholder patterns case-insensitively, skips no-text-frame shapes and empty-stripped-text shapes. Check 2 walks a:rPr[@sz] XML elements, reports fonts below 1200 units as errors, excludes footer-region shapes (top > 90% of slide height). File compiles cleanly.
handoff_summary.remaining: Downstream beads handle: CLI command wiring (verify command in cli.py), checks 3–5 (overflow, text clipping, shape overlap), tests in test_verify.py, and SKILL.md QA checklist update.
handoff_summary.risks: Check 2 uses shape._element.iter() on python-pptx internals — stable but technically private API. The footer threshold is computed once per slide from prs.slide_height; shapes with top=None are not excluded (they fall through the None guard and are checked).
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should write tests/test_verify.py covering all 7 acceptance criteria for checks 1 and 2. Subsequent beads add checks 3–5, CLI wiring, and SKILL.md update.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — verify.py is a new file. CLI bead (cli.py) will import from this module; ensure the import path matches .apm/skills/pypptx/pypptx/ops/verify.py.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used shape.text_frame.text (which concatenates all runs/paragraphs) for check 1 rather than per-paragraph matching — this matches the spec intent of whole-shape placeholder detection. Used _element.iter() for check 2 to avoid re-parsing already-loaded XML, consistent with how python-pptx exposes the underlying lxml/ElementTree tree.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No tests written by this bead (developer scope). Test bead must cover: check 1 fires for each of 9 patterns; check 1 does not fire on no-text-frame shape; check 1 does not fire on empty-stripped shape; check 2 fires below 1200 units; check 2 does not fire at exactly 1200; check 2 skips footer-region shapes.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Check 2 does not deduplicate multiple a:rPr elements within the same run (e.g. direct and inherited formatting); each sz attribute hit generates a separate error entry. This is consistent with the spec which says 'walk all a:rPr[@sz] elements'.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — verify.py is a new file. CLI bead (cli.py) will import from this module; ensure the import path matches .apm/skills/pypptx/pypptx/ops/verify.py.

execution_history.summary: Created pypptx/ops/verify.py implementing verify_pptx() with check 1 (unfilled placeholder hint text, 9 patterns, case-insensitive) and check 2 (font size below 12pt / 1200 units, footer-region exclusion at top > 90% of slide height). Returns {errors, warnings, slide_count}.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
